clean_story: Search for meaning.  None found—keep searching. "Absurdism" names the clash between our hunger for purpose and a silent universe that offers no answers. Like shouting into a canyon and hearing only wind. The idea took shape in 1942 with Albert Camus’ The Myth of Sisyphus. He asked:If life’s struggles lead nowhere—like a boulder rolling back downhill—why not quit?Absurdism replies:Because the struggle itself can be the point. Imagine building a sandcastle knowing the tide will erase it. The joy isn’t in permanence—it’s in the shaping, the silly defiance of the inevitable. Camus called this "revolt. "Not against gods or fate—but against the lie that life owes us meaning. You eat ice cream on a Tuesday. You dance alone to a song you hate. Small acts of freedom in a world that doesn’t care. Three related subjects are existential rebellion, stoic acceptance, and dark humor.
